steps.title: Use a Desmos table regression
steps.content: This is a line-of-best-fit update question, so the fastest reliable method is to read the plotted points into a Desmos table. From the scatterplot, data set A is approximately $(2,12)$, $(3,22)$, $(4,28)$, $(5,41)$, and $(6,47)$. Add the new point $(9,52)$ to make data set B.

steps.title: Run the regression row
steps.content: Enter the $x$-values as $x_1=[2,3,4,5,6,9]$ and the $y$-values as $y_1=[12,22,28,41,47,52]$. Then type the custom regression $y_1\sim m x_1+b$. Desmos gives $m\approx5.86$, so the slope $s$ of the new line of best fit is closest to $5.9$.

steps.title: Check the direction
steps.content: This answer also makes sense without exact calculation. The old line predicts $-5.1+8.7(9)=73.2$ pounds at $x=9$, but the new point is only $(9,52)$, far below that trend. Adding a low right-side point must pull the best-fit slope down from $8.7$. The only answer choice near the regression result and below $8.7$ is $5.9$, so choice A is correct.
